text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Eleanor Watts  
St Cuthbert’s University Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, St Cuthbert’s Way, London SW3 8AB  

Dear Dr Eleanor Watts,  

Maria Bennett, DOB: 14-07-1992, Hospital No: H462917 NHS No. 9087712456  
12 Brookview Close, London, W6 2JP  

Reason for contact: Pre-conception counselling and digital access to clinic services. 

I met with Maria Bennett today for pre-conception counselling. We discussed practical steps to manage clinic appointments and secure messaging via the NHS App. 

Guidance provided: 
- NHS App registration: Maria Bennett will download the NHS App and verify identity using photo ID or GP-linked verification.  
- Linking hospital records: Within the App, select “Manage services” then “Hospitals” and add St Cuthbert’s University Hospital to view appointments and letters when available.  
- Appointments: Future neurology appointments will appear under “Appointments”. Reminders can be enabled, and requests to reschedule can be submitted through the App where supported.  
- Messaging: Non-urgent questions can be sent via “Messages” to the Neurology service if this option is visible. Urgent concerns should be directed to the on-call service via the hospital switchboard or NHS 111 as appropriate.  
- Test results and letters: Clinic summaries and test results will be visible in “Messages” or “Record” once released.  
- Notifications: Enable notifications in phone settings and within the App to receive appointment confirmations and clinician replies.  
- Accessibility: The App supports larger text and language translation on compatible devices. 

Plan:  
- Provide the patient with the neurology service access code if required for messaging activation.  
- Share NHS App help link and hospital digital support line details.  
- Arrange routine follow-up as per standard pathway; administrative queries to be made via the App where possible. 

Please contact me if you need any further information. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: I reviewed the entire letter. It addresses pre-conception counselling and instructions for using the NHS App for appointments, messaging, and digital access. There is no mention of seizures, seizure frequency, or seizure-free duration anywhere in the text. No numeric or qualitative seizure information is provided.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter providing guidance on using the NHS App for epilepsy appointments and messages

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
